---
title: "Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict"
subtitle: "A Cross-Country Analysis · January–April 2026"
author: "Andrea Busolo"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 7
    fig-dpi: 150
    embed-resources: true
---

## Introduction

This analysis examines how newspapers across **11 countries and regions** frame and
report on the Iran–Israel conflict during the period **January 4 – April 4, 2026**.

We collected articles from 55 major outlets using the **GDELT Global Knowledge Graph**,
extracted full text via web scraping, and ran multilingual sentiment analysis using
**XLM-RoBERTa** — a transformer model trained on 100+ languages. This allows us to
analyze Italian, Spanish, French, German, and Portuguese articles in their *native language*
without losing linguistic nuance through translation.

### Countries and outlets covered

| Region | Country | Language | Outlets |
|--------|---------|----------|---------|
| North America | USA | English | NYT, Washington Post, Fox News, CNN, WSJ |
| North America | Canada | English | Globe and Mail, Toronto Star, National Post, CBC, CTV |
| Europe | UK | English | The Guardian, BBC, The Times, Daily Mail, The Independent |
| Europe | Italy | Italian | Corriere della Sera, La Repubblica, La Stampa, Il Sole 24 Ore, ANSA |
| Europe | Spain | Spanish | El País, El Mundo, ABC, La Vanguardia, El Confidencial |
| Europe | France | French | Le Monde, Le Figaro, Libération, Le Parisien, BFM TV |
| Europe | Germany | German | Der Spiegel, Die Zeit, FAZ, Süddeutsche Zeitung, Bild |
| South America | Brazil | Portuguese | Folha, O Globo, Estadão, UOL, Veja |
| South America | Argentina | Spanish | La Nación, Clarín, Infobae, Página 12, Perfil |
| Middle East | Al-Jazeera | English | aljazeera.com |
| Middle East | Israel | English | Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Ynet, Israel National News |

### Methodology

1. **Data collection**: GDELT 2.0 GKG queried via Google BigQuery, filtered by source domain and Iran/Israel/War themes.
2. **Article scraping**: Full text extracted with `trafilatura`; paywalled articles use available snippets.
3. **Sentiment model**: `cardiffnlp/xlm-roberta-base-sentiment-multilingual` — 3-class classification (positive / neutral / negative). Long articles chunked into 510-token windows with 50-token overlap; probabilities aggregated by token-weighted average.
4. **Validation**: BERT sentiment cross-validated against GDELT's built-in tone scores.

---

{{< include 01_overview.qmd >}}

---

{{< include 02_sentiment.qmd >}}

---

{{< include 03_words.qmd >}}

---

{{< include 04_timeline.qmd >}}

---

## Conclusions

*[To be written after running the analysis — summarize key findings about sentiment differences across geographies, notable outliers, and temporal patterns.]*

## Reproducibility

All code is available at [github.com/andbbus/war-sentiment](https://github.com/andbbus/war-sentiment).

To reproduce:

```bash
# 1. Install Python dependencies
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

# 2. Collect GDELT data (requires GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS)
python -m src.collect.gdelt_query

# 3. Scrape full article text
python -m src.collect.scraper

# 4. Preprocess
python -m src.preprocess.pipeline

# 5. Run sentiment inference
python -m src.sentiment.inference

# 6. Validate and export summary
python -m src.sentiment.export

# 7. Render Quarto report
quarto render analysis/report.qmd
```
